comments.comment: The change in arsenic quantities released to air is a result of reduced operational hours of two dust collectors between 2020 and 2019. In particular, dust collector D1 was only operational in 2019. D1 has an elevated arsenic emission rate compared to the other dust collectors on site.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: A regulatory stack emission testing (SET) program was conducted between July 9 and August 14, 2021. Data from the SET program was used to update emission rates for use in emission calculations. Additionally, the method to determine vehicle kilometers travelled (VKT) for heavy equipment (i.e., haul trucks) was updated to reflect changes to operational reporting in 2021.

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
#6 fuel oil consumption in boilers was 790 kiloliters less in 2022 compared to 2021, resulting in reduced air emissions. In addition to emissions from #6 fuel oil combustion, releases to air are calculated based on hours of operation for dryers and dust collectors. Operation hours vary by year, depending on operational requirements. Emissions from dryers are calculated using emission rates for normal and reclaimed concentrate, thus annual production of concentrate and reclaimed concentrate impact emission calculations. In 2022, annual production was comparable to that of 2021, but there was less time spent on reclaim concentrate since less material was sent to the reclaim pile, resulting in less concentrate to reclaim and reduced releases to air. Additionally, variability amongst the nine operational dust collectors on site resulted in reduced releases to air.

Effluent volume was reduced in 2022 compared to 2021, resulting in reduced releases to receiving waters (Wabush Lake).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
